summary: DHH highlights how a developer named Ben Stiglitz built an early bug tracker skeleton for the Rails project in just three hours, with no prior Rails training and only one week of Ruby experience. The post uses this anecdote to evangelize Rails' ease of adoption, noting that the community had outgrown wiki-based bug tracking. DHH frames the story as proof that anyone can pick up Ruby on Rails quickly, especially developers coming from PHP.
key_points: The Rails community needed to move from wiki-based bug tracking to a dedicated bug tracker as the project grew
A developer with zero Rails experience and one week of Ruby built a working skeleton in three hours
DHH uses the anecdote as a recruitment pitch for Ruby on Rails adoption, targeting PHP developers specifically
money_quotes: Within three hours. With no prior Rails training. After having played around with Ruby for one week.
If I can do it, he can do it, then you certainly can too!
Additional respect for being a PHP refugee like myself.
key_insight: Rails' low barrier to entry is demonstrated by a newcomer building a functional bug tracker in hours, not days—serving as both a community milestone and an evangelism tool.
